header.mission: Reduce friction and improve conversion from approval to funding while increasing customer self-service.

executive_summary.priority_last_quarter: reduce friction across the approval-to-funding journey, with deliberate focus on three areas: simplifying document submission, removing manual review bottlenecks to reduce cycle time, and expanding digital self-service to reduce assisted support demand. Effort was concentrated on the highest drop-off moments in the journey -- particularly document-related friction between approval and funding -- where customer confusion and redundant process steps were suppressing conversion.
executive_summary.outcomes: Digital self-service adoption continued to grow across the approval-to-funding journey, with 65% of customers using digital channels to track status, submit documents, and resolve issues without assisted support (up from 58% at Q1 start). Funding conversion improved from 62% to 65%, document drop-off fell from 18% to 12%, and approval-to-funding cycle time was reduced from 19 to 18.5 days.
learning_points: Clarity, not speed, drives customer behavior. Removing redundant document requests and providing clearer instructions had a disproportionately large impact on both conversion (+3 pts) and NPS (+1.5 pts). The primary mechanism was reduced customer uncertainty -- not process acceleration. This challenges the assumption that cycle time is the primary lever for funding conversion.

Reducing friction in a small number of high-impact moments delivers outsized gains. Focused changes at the document submission stage drove improvements across funding rate, drop-off, and experience simultaneously -- confirming the approval-to-funding journey is won or lost at discrete friction points, not across the whole flow.

Automation alone does not solve for complexity. Partial automation of manual review reduced queue volume by ~15%, but complex cases still required full manual intervention. The structural constraint is case variability, not automation maturity -- a distinction that should shape Q2 investment in early validation rather than further queue automation.

Proactive visibility reduces support demand. Introducing proactive status tracking and clearer next steps drove a 10% reduction in call volume alongside a 7-point improvement in self-service rate -- confirming that customers escalate when uncertain, not merely when dissatisfied.
next_opportunities: Scale document simplification to more complex customer scenarios, where drop-off and friction remain highest and Q1 gains have not yet reached.
Further reduce approval-to-funding cycle time from 18.5 to ~18 days by expanding early validation and straight-through processing for standard cases.
Extend self-service capability to edge cases and non-standard journeys, which currently fall back to assisted support and represent the next frontier of call volume reduction.

okrs.upstream_linkage: Supports PBB OKR to increase digital engagement and reduce assisted service cost. This OKR addresses the primary conversion gap between approval and funding by eliminating friction at the moments customers most commonly disengage.
okrs.objective: Shift customers to active owners of their funding journey through friction elimination.

key_results.text: Increase funding conversion rate from 65% to 68%
Reduce document-related drop-off from 12% to less than 10%
key_results.support_needed: Alignment with Upstream Approval and Credit Crews to reduce redundant upstream document requirements

key_results.text: Enable 70% of customers to complete document submission without assisted support
key_results.support_needed: Digital Channels for consistent action and guidance presentation

okrs.upstream_linkage: Supports PBB operational efficiency OKR. This OKR exists to reduce manual handling cost and cycle time, enabling higher throughput without proportional resource growth.
okrs.objective: Build a faster, more automated operational backbone for the funding journey.

key_results.text: Reduce approval-to-funding cycle time from 18.5 to 18 days
Reduce manual review volume by an additional 20%
key_results.status: -15% achieved in Q1; further reduction targeted
key_results.support_needed: Enterprise Credit to approve early-stage validation rules and tolerance thresholds for complex cases

key_results.text: Increase straight-through processing for standard cases to 75%
key_results.support_needed: Credit and Technology teams to enable automated early validation

okrs.upstream_linkage: Supports PBB digital engagement and customer experience OKRs. This OKR scales Q1 self-service gains to a broader range of customer scenarios, reducing structural dependency on assisted channels.
okrs.objective: Make digital self-service the default across the approval-to-funding journey.

key_results.text: Reduce inbound call volume by an additional 10% on top of Q1 reduction
key_results.status: -10% achieved in Q1; further reduction targeted
key_results.support_needed: Cross-domain alignment on consistent status messaging and next-step guidance

key_results.text: Improve NPS by +2 pts over Q1 baseline

financial_impact.metric: Additional financial KPI to be confirmed

dependencies.constraint: Early validation and document condition surfacing for the approval-to-funding journey
dependencies.required_action: Upstream Approval and Credit Crews to expose funding-critical conditions earlier in the journey, reducing downstream document requests
dependencies.decision_by: End of Q2 (to enable early validation rollout and prevent continued drop-off in complex cases)

dependencies.constraint: Automation capability for complex and edge-case manual review scenarios
dependencies.required_action: Credit and Technology to assess and prioritise automation tooling for non-standard cases; current scope covers standard flows only
dependencies.decision_by: Q2 (to avoid manual review becoming a binding constraint on cycle time reduction)

dependencies.constraint: Fragmented end-to-end ownership across approval, credit, and funding teams
dependencies.required_action: Cross-domain leadership to clarify accountability boundaries and establish a single coordination point for end-to-end journey decisions
dependencies.decision_by: Q2 planning cycle (required to unblock initiatives with multi-crew dependencies)

risks.blocker: "Funding-ready" lacks a consistent, shared definition across teams and channels, leading to inconsistent customer guidance, cautious decision-making, and duplicated indicators that create confusion rather than clarity. If unresolved, this will limit the impact of self-service improvements and create quality risk as we scale.
asks: Endorse a single, authoritative definition of "funding-ready" as the primary decision point across all channels and teams.
Support consistent application of this definition, including retiring familiar but duplicative indicators where they conflict.

risks.blocker: Improvement gains risk plateauing if edge cases are not addressed as a deliberate priority. Q1 wins were concentrated in standard journeys. Edge cases -- which carry higher manual effort, longer cycle time, and higher drop-off -- are not yet within scope of existing initiatives and will become the binding constraint on further conversion and NPS improvement.
asks: Align on prioritising end-to-end journey optimisation over siloed, within-team efficiency gains.
Direct relevant domains (Credit, Approval, Digital) to treat edge-case resolution as a shared, cross-domain objective rather than a secondary backlog item.

initiatives.initiative: Document Simplification Expansion -- extend simplified document submission and clearer guidance to complex customer scenarios beyond the standard approval-to-funding flow
initiatives.outcomes: Reduced drop-off in complex cases; improved conversion among previously high-friction customer segments; fewer assisted contacts for document queries
initiatives.relevant_okrs: O1: KR1 (conversion to 68%), KR2 (drop-off <10%), KR3 (70% unassisted submission)
initiatives.risks_dependencies: Dependency on Upstream Approval and Credit to reduce redundant upstream conditions; requires Digital Channels for consistent experience delivery

initiatives.initiative: Early Validation and Automation -- expand pre-funding validation and introduce straight-through processing for standard cases to reduce manual review volume and cycle time
initiatives.outcomes: Cycle time reduced to 18 days; manual review volume reduced by additional 20%; straight-through processing at 75% for standard cases
initiatives.relevant_okrs: O2: KR1 (cycle time), KR2 (manual review), KR3 (straight-through processing)
initiatives.risks_dependencies: Requires Enterprise Credit to approve validation rules and tolerances for early-stage processing; complexity of edge cases may limit automation scope

initiatives.initiative: Self-Service Expansion for Complex Cases -- extend proactive status tracking, next-step guidance, and self-resolution capability to non-standard journeys currently defaulting to assisted support
initiatives.outcomes: Increased self-service rate to 72%; additional 10% reduction in call volume; NPS improvement of +2 pts
initiatives.relevant_okrs: O3: KR1 (self-service rate), KR2 (call volume), KR3 (NPS)
initiatives.risks_dependencies: Cross-domain alignment required on consistent messaging and journey design for edge cases; Digital platform dependencies to be confirmed
